message:
feat(abi): port 20 nis_* CRUD + directory + misc stubs (bd-lk6tq)

Finish the NIS+ libnsl parity work. Twenty more nis_* fail-safe
entries — the remaining lookup/CRUD/directory/diagnostic surface.

Functions returning *mut nis_result return NULL (matches the
documented out-of-memory failure path so well-behaved NIS+ callers
already check):
  - nis_lookup / nis_list / nis_add / nis_add_entry / nis_modify /
    nis_modify_entry / nis_remove / nis_remove_entry /
    nis_first_entry / nis_next_entry / nis_checkpoint

Object-array returns (NULL):
  - nis_getnames(name) -> **char (would expand a partially-qualified
    name to a NULL-terminated array of FQ candidates)
  - nis_getservlist(dir) -> **directory_obj
  - nis_read_obj(filename) -> *nis_object

Directory ops returning nis_error == NIS_NAMEUNREACHABLE (5):
  - nis_mkdir(name, *server)
  - nis_rmdir(name, *server)
  - nis_servstate(server, *state, num, **out)
  - nis_stats(server, *info, num, **out)

Misc:
  - nis_ping(name, utime, *dirobj) — void no-op
  - nis_write_obj(filename, *obj) -> bool_t = 0 (FALSE)

Tests: 8 abi tests covering NULL returns across the 11 *result
functions, the 3 object-array helpers, and read_obj; NIS_NAMEUNREACHABLE
returns from mkdir/rmdir/servstate/stats; ping crash-safety with
NULL and arbitrary args; write_obj zero return.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
